coupling.rationale: ipc.ts at 503 lines is becoming a God module hosting PKCE OAuth, safeStorage, and API proxy alongside MCP, agents, plugins, and OS handlers. Fix-B and Fix-E both land in the same 3 files. Precedent for extraction exists (desktop-menu-actions.ts). RepositoryCache gets a GitHub-specific method in its generic interface.

debuggability.rationale: safeStorage error messages are clear. GitHubProxyError has structured fields. OAuth state mismatch detection works. But: github-oauth-start returns undocumented UUID, API proxy errors use JSON.stringify inside Error (IPC serialization buries structure), no OAuth flow timeout, no OAuth cancellation mechanism.

convergence.rationale: Revives dead code (parseGitHubRemote had 0 callers). Leverages existing patterns: Effect service, IPC handlers, ElectronAPI extension, produce_fragment for shared files. New code organized into github/ module. Minor: ipc.ts continues growing instead of being split into domain modules.

surface_area.rationale: Major surface expansion: 6 new ElectronAPI methods, 1 new DesktopMenuAction union member affecting 9 files across 2 packages, RepositoryCache interface addition, new @opencode-ai/core/github module export, new deep-links.ts export. Each is a permanent API commitment. githubSetToken has no format validation.

testability.rationale: fix-A is well-tested (12 test cases). fix-C is testable with mocked Octokit. Everything else requires Electron runtime: OAuth flow (browser + deep link), safeStorage (system keychain), IPC proxy (net.fetch). PR UI requires DOM. Heavy reliance on manual testing.

error_clarity.rationale: Good: safeStorage error, GitHubProxyError fields, OAuth state mismatch, github.noAuth key. Problems: API proxy throws JSON.stringify'd errors through IPC (renderer must parse .message), github-oauth-callback returns bare true, no OAuth timeout errors, no OAuth cancellation flow.

reversibility.rationale: Rollback plan documented with clear order. fix-A, fix-C, fix-D are independently revertible. Concern: fix-B and fix-E share 3 files (ipc.ts, preload/types.ts, preload/index.ts) — handlers are inlined, so reverting one requires surgical line identification. DesktopMenuAction union cleanup must sync with ElectronAPI type cleanup.

findings_filed.summary: ipc.ts God module — extract GitHub IPC handlers into separate module
githubSetToken lacks format validation and has no confirmation dialog
API proxy error serialization through IPC is fragile (JSON.stringify in Error.message)
13 i18n keys only in en.ts — will show undefined in 15 other locales
parseGitHubRemote added to RepositoryCache interface pollutes generic service with GitHub-specific method

conditions_for_approval: Extract GitHub IPC handlers into packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts (separate module, registered by registerIpcHandlers)
Add GitHub PAT format validation to githubSetToken (main process or preload side)
Replace JSON.stringify Error pattern in github-api-proxy with structured return object for IPC-safe error propagation
Add github.* keys to 15 non-English locale files or implement fallback in LanguageProvider
follow_up_issues: Token revocation — githubClearToken should call GitHub /applications/{client_id}/token DELETE endpoint
Cross-process cleanup — no mechanism for coordinated token cleanup across OpenCode server + Electron main processes
OAuth flow timeout — pendingOAuth Map entries leak if user never completes browser flow
Enterprise GitHub support — ALLOWED_GITHUB_HOSTNAMES excludes self-hosted GitHub instances

key_risks_assessment.wiring_dead_code_back_in: parseGitHubRemote is sound. Uses well-tested helper functions (normalizeRepositoryInput, parseRepositoryReference). 12 test cases pass. No issues.
key_risks_assessment.ipc_security: Token is safeStorage-encrypted at rest, never exposed to renderer (attached server-side in proxy), store is reserved. Weak spot: githubSetToken in preload bridge accepts any string without format validation.
key_risks_assessment.account_delete_cascade: Not addressed in plan. githubClearToken removes from electron-store but doesn't revoke token server-side. Cross-process cleanup (OpenCode server + Electron) not designed. Out of scope for this lane but important gap.
key_risks_assessment.lane_boundary: Lane is broad — 5 fixes across 16+ files covering OAuth, PR management, CI/CD, API proxy, UI. At upper limit of 'narrow'. Could be split into auth (fix-B+fix-E) and PR (fix-A+fix-C+fix-D) sub-lanes.
